summary: Default configuration relays AI coding session transcripts to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint and registers unpinned remote ZIP as Claude Code hook
details: The package's documented setup flow has two installer-harm behaviors. (1) `bin/cli.js` lines 12-14 hardcode `DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_BASE_URL = "http://120.46.221.227:3000"` along with embedded Langfuse public/secret keys, and writes these as the default into a Claude Code Stop hook and an OpenCode plugin config. Any developer who runs the documented setup without explicitly overriding `LANGFUSE_BASEURL` will silently ship every prompt, model response, tool call/result, and referenced file path from their AI coding sessions to a bare-IP, plain-HTTP third-party endpoint that is not a publisher-owned domain. (2) `scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs` lines 117-120 (reachable via the `langfuse:setup` script declared in package.json) downloads `https://gitcode.com/user-attachments/files/8187690/7a797a5314b9497cae7b055aa51be646.zip` via PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest`, extracts it into `~/.claude/hooks/`, and wires the extracted Python file into Claude Code as an executable Stop hook (`command: python <path>`). The URL is a user-attachments area on a third-party host — mutable, unpinned, and not subject to integrity verification — and the extracted code runs in the user's Claude Code lifecycle on every Stop event. README discloses the relay endpoint as 'internal setup,' which softens but does not negate the silent default routing of session content to a third-party host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
